metric_value: Two converging worlds (CE and PC) meet in a broad spectrum of possibilities; six player categories: broadband, point-product CE, game, PC/peripheral, display, software, new entrants; semiconductors as arms merchants
notes: Anchor framework of the entire deck

metric_value: Target is mass consumer market (not geek early adopters); no singular solution; information interface need not be PC; single system management/content catalog accessible remotely; multiple content/computing centers in home; consumer choice over redundancy; industry standards with acceptable proprietary integration; plug-and-play imperative; upgradeability imperative
Identify end-user requirements; work with established and emergent vendors; leverage other Aberdeen practices for enabling-tech elements; hypothesize products and approaches; test and refine
DVD/CD/MP3 player + cable receiver + audio/video receiver/amp + surround sound + digital theater sound
BMC 8000: network+firewall, PVR, MP3 jukebox. BMC 9000 adds multiple-TV support, dual-tuner PVR, optional HD, optional DVD
WebStar: Firewall+Router+Networking+802.11b+HPNA 2.0+USB 1.0+Ethernet. Explorer 8000: pause live TV, dual-record, picture-in-picture, PVR, 50 hours storage

metric_name: Pace Micro Home Media Center with Philips
metric_value: Hard drive; two 802.11b video streams; one wireline video stream; thin-client MPEG-2 decoder at each TV
notes: Early multi-stream home video distribution architecture

metric_value: 10/100 Ethernet + USB + HomePNA 2.0 + unlimited video channels with surround + VOD + EPG + Internet/email + interactive services

metric_name: Time Warner Cable home networking trial pricing
metric_value: $50 install + $9.95/mo (cable modem + gateway + firewall + HomePNA for 3 devices); 802.11b option $30 additional + $14.95/mo
notes: First retail home-networking bundle from major MSO

metric_value: HomePNA $249.95; 802.11b $449.95; DSL fee separate; customer self-install

metric_name: Pace DSL 4000 and IP500 gateway coverage
metric_value: DSL 4000 enables short-haul DSL MPEG-1+2 video; IP500 enables MPEG-4 over longer DSL estimated to cover 70% of rural areas

MSOs are financially constrained: capital equipment (STB) must be very cost-effective; profitability is marginal; strong interest in new fee services and content consumption expansion; tremendous rental consumer access; limited field skills, no centralized support, marginal consumer credibility
notes: Strategic assessment of cable operator posture

metric_value: 3-6 month design cycles leveraging feature-rich semiconductor reference designs; Chinese contract manufacturing; low overhead; fluid organization — versus historic CE: 2-year cycles, backward integration, heavy overhead, ponderous structures
Broad DCEIT industry forces will make share and profitability loss inevitable for incumbents who fail to adapt
Closed online gaming network; $49/year fee including VOIP headset; gaming fees set by game software companies

metric_name: Game console platform as media-server base
metric_value: Game console platforms have possible media-server base, high-speed graphics rendering, Internet access, DVD play, large hard drive, saturating teenage market, strong channel presence, razor-and-blades pricing suggesting potential hardware subsidies to improve position

Success of narrowband interactive gaming suggests pent-up demand for broadband format; key requirement beyond connectivity is display/processor (P-4) in home; EA platform-agnostic and potential key strategic partner

Increasing yields of large flat-panel displays will make technology economic in consumer products; ability to integrate digital electronics on display substrates will enable display-centric computers and video products; display makers may become end-product manufacturers as value-add grows

metric_name: Intel Personal Video Player reference design
metric_value: Four-inch color display; 30 GB storage for 100 hours of video; DRM; 3+ hours battery life

metric_name: Intel Advanced Home Media Platform reference design
metric_value: Broadband gateway + DVD playback + PVR + TV reception + MP3 + interactive gaming + content storage + VOD + home networking; Linux/embedded XP/CE OS support
STB chipset with PVR capability; single-chip ADSL router/bridge with USB and Ethernet

metric_name: Wind River Tornado for Home Gateways
metric_value: Integrated software platform and development environment for broadband access gateways: RTOS + embedded web server + Ethernet + USB + xDSL + DOCSIS + voice/multimedia + home networking options (802.11a/b/g, HomePNA, Bluetooth, HomePlug)
24-27 GB discs versus 4.7 GB current; likely available 2003 in read/record products
Potential inexpensive 100 Mbit short-range wireless; may be technology of choice for streaming video
notes: UWB prediction — partially materialized as wireless USB/WiGig

metric_value: Open systems/interoperability required for unbundled offerings; box-level products commoditized rapidly; DRM and effort to program media servers with legacy content may create first-mover edge; expensive recurring-fee business models for undifferentiated content will become bundled give-aways

As many as four supply-chain models required: traditional ship-to-stock via multi-tier distribution; rapid replenishment from central stock; configure-to-order; web-based sales — mapped to product axes value/volatility/configurability
notes: Supply chain framework unique to v6

metric_name: What CE companies must get right
metric_value: Good user experience for all products; competitive box-level products; value-added systems of products enabling value pricing (seamless plug-and-play, superb customer support, extended asset life through field upgrades/downloads for a fee)

This v6 deck (Sep 2002) is the final iteration of a practice-overview presentation evolved through DCT-Board-Presentation (Jul 2002, board-level summary), DCT-practice-jul-26 (Jul 26 2002, full deck with market sizing: US CE market ~$94B 2001, forecast $118B 2005 @ 6% CAGR, home networking 58% CAGR, home gateways 73% CAGR), and DCEIT-Practice (Aug-Sep 2002, DCEIT rebrand). Prior versions archived but not separately ingested per user decision.
notes: Metadata capturing three prior versions not separately parsed

metric_value: Companion Nov 1 2002 webinar (dct-webinar-nov-2002) extended this framework with deep-dives on Mobile Convergence Devices, Media Servers, and Home Networking; promotional announcement archived as dct-webinar-announcement-2002-11
